title: calliandra glyphoxylon
intro: Calliandra glyphoxylon, also known as "fairy duster" or "powderpuff," is a flowering plant native to Central and South America, characterized by its pink or white powdery flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, to treat various health conditions. Its health benefits include soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and reducing fever, among others. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, tea, or powder, and is often used in traditional medicine, although its use should be guided by a healthcare professional.
